why_not: combines routes.json and secret-bearing state/secrets writes without current necessity
current packets do not prove route add must be fused with token/materialization work
local token creation exists only in external-models start --json
with zero routes and zero models, token-only cannot resolve unknown provider for configured model
would widen into secret-bearing state mutation earlier than necessary
no standalone provider/profile mutation command surface exists
provider mapping is embedded in route payload and therefore route-add/update owned

rollback_expectations: restore routes.json byte-for-byte if post-add proof remains blocked or contradictory
why: maps directly to canonical mutation surface external-models routes add --json --file|--stdin
narrowest write domain is routes.json only
addresses the earliest machine-carried gap: routes_count=0 and models_count=0 from local_routes_registry
does not require secret-bearing token materialization or launcher work

ui_boundary.why_not_blocking_here: this contour adjudicates runtime repair scope over canonical CLI owner surfaces, not browser UI admission
